name: Speedy Cash
description_short: Speedy Cash is a storefront short-term lender offering payday loans up to $425, title loans and lines of credit up to $4,000, plus check cashing and money services.
description_long:
Speedy Cash is a national retail consumer lending chain with neighborhood storefronts across the US, including this Memphis, TN location on Summer Avenue. The brand operates under Community Choice Financial and offers walk-in financial services targeting customers who need fast access to cash outside the traditional banking system. This location is open six days a week with extended evening hours on Mondays and Fridays.

The Memphis store offers three distinct lending products. Payday Loans are capped at $425 and are due on or around the borrower's next payday — the simplest, fastest product for small, short-term needs. Lines of Credit go up to $4,000 and function as revolving credit: as you repay principal and interest, your available credit limit is restored, offering flexibility for ongoing or unpredictable expenses. Title Loans also go up to $4,000 and use the borrower's vehicle as collateral, requiring an in-store vehicle appraisal and a clear title. Beyond lending, the store offers check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, and Green Dot Visa Debit Cards.

Speedy Cash differentiates itself from purely online lenders through the in-person experience — customers can ask questions, review loan terms face-to-face, and complete the full application process on-site. Required documents are listed clearly for each product before you visit. Both online and in-store application paths are available, providing some flexibility for customers who want to start the process remotely.

Speedy Cash primarily serves borrowers who lack access to traditional bank credit and need quick cash. These are high-cost lending products: payday loans in particular carry APRs that can exceed 300–400% when annualized, and title loans carry the risk of vehicle repossession if not repaid. The payday loan cap of $425 is modest and may not cover larger emergencies. Consumers should review Tennessee's posted Rates & Terms carefully, compare total repayment costs, and consider lower-cost alternatives before borrowing.
pros: Payday loans available up to $425 with same-day in-store funding
Revolving line of credit up to $4,000 — credit restores as you repay
Title loans up to $4,000 for borrowers who own a vehicle outright
Ancillary financial services: check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, Green Dot Debit Cards
Extended hours Monday and Friday until 7pm; Saturday hours available
Both in-store and online application options offered
Document requirements listed upfront for each loan type before you visit
cons: Payday loans capped at $425 — not suitable for larger emergency expenses
High-cost products typical of the payday/title loan sector; APRs not disclosed on this page
Title loans require in-store vehicle appraisal and carry repossession risk if unpaid
Closed Sundays, limiting access one day per week
Store description references 'Check Into Cash' branding, suggesting potential brand inconsistency
best_for: Borrowers needing fast cash under $500 to bridge a gap until payday
Vehicle owners seeking short-term loans of $1,000–$4,000 using their car as collateral
Unbanked or underbanked consumers who need check cashing or money transfer services
Customers who prefer face-to-face service and want to ask questions before signing
diagnosis: Speedy Cash is best for consumers who need small-dollar cash quickly and prefer in-person service, especially for payday loans under $425 or title loans backed by a vehicle. The main caveat is cost: these are among the most expensive forms of consumer credit available, and borrowers who can qualify for a credit union payday alternative loan (PAL) or personal loan will pay significantly less.
services: Vehicle appraisal for title loan collateral
In-person loan document review and signing
